rulings.Case Title: PRECISION INDUSTRIAL CONSTRUCTORS, INC. VS. SNOWFLAKE MILL INVESTORS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production, And From Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 3, 2014, Line 8, PLAINTIFF PRECISION INDUSTRIAL CONSTRUCTORS, INC.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production, And Form Interrogatories. The motion is granted. Defendants are ordered to produce all nonprivileged documents responsive to Requests 40 and 41 that relate to any of the following: (1) the matters alleged in the Second Amended Complaint and cross-complaint; (2) work involving hazardous material that was performed by any contractor of defendants at the facility; or (3) the release of any hazardous material at the facility that reasonably might have been caused by any contractor of defendants. Defendants should also produce documents sufficient to identify the name, address and phone number of each contractor that defendants have hired to work at the facility. The motion is granted as to Requests 55, 56, 59, 62, 63, 64, and 87. Defendants shall produce all nonprivileged documents responsive to these requests, except that defendants may redact the confidential financial information of the nonparties. The motion is granted as to Requests 57 and 60. Defendants shall produce all nonprivileged documents responsive to these requests that are related to matters alleged in the Second Amended Complaint and cross-complaint or that assets that PIC purchased. The motion is granted as to Requests 70, 73, and 74 and defendants must produce all responsive documents. The motion to compel further response to form interrogatory 3.7 is granted. All further responses are due by November 20, 2014. The moving party did not seek monetary sanctions. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: WATERFALL WELLNESS CO-OPERATIVE, INC. VS. GREGORY SCHOEPP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, To Compel Compliance And For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Its Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 3, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT GREGORY SCHOEPP Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, To Compel Compliance And For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Its Counsel. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted. Waterfall Wellness shall, within ten court days, serve verified written responses without objections to Defendant's Request for Production of Documents, Set One--Requests 1 through 16; and shall produce all responsive documents to Requests 1 through 20 within that time period. Sanctions are awarded against Waterfall Wellness and its attorney, Paul Perdue, in the amount of $2,400. See Corns v. Miller (1986) 181 Cal. App. 3d 195, 201. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN BUFORD VS. ASHBURY GENERAL CONTRACTING & ENGINEERING, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 3, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANTS ASHBURY GENERAL CONTRACTING & ENGINEERING, INC., ANTHONY BYRNE, KEVIN BORN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. As to the negligence cause of action, the duty to "act in a reasonable manner" arises from the contract. (Complaint, pars. 13, 20.) Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege a duty that does not arise from contract. (See Aas v. Sup. Ct. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 627, 643, "A person may not ordinarily recover in tort for the breach of duties that merely restate contractual obligations.") As to the intentional misrepresentation cause of action, a fraud complaint must plead facts that "show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered." (Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege intentional misrepresentation with the requisite amount of specificity. As to the violation of Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 17200 cause of action, because Plaintiff has not adequately alleged a fraudulent business practice, he has not adequately alleged unfair competition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: IGOR KRAYCHIK VS. EXIGEN (USA), INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 3, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT EXIGEN EBIT, INC.'S Petition To Compel Arbitration. Motion granted. Plaintiff produces no evidence that Shenkman lacked the authority to execute the Offer Letter. In fact, evidence produced by Plaintiff tends to support the opposite conclusion-that is, the Offer Letter itself bears Shenkman's name, signature, and title of "Acting CEO," all set forth on Defendant's letterhead, which is all indicative of Shenkman's authority, and is further supported by Kolt's declaration. Additionally, the arbitration agreement was not procedurally unconscionable. The evidence presented establishes that the employment contract was negotiable as to job title, bonus, salary, moving allowance, and start date (Kraychik Decl., Ex. 1), and Plaintiff fails to present evidence that any other provision in the Offer Letter was not similarly negotiable. Furthermore, a failure to include or provide the arbitration rules referenced in the agreement alone is insufficient to support a finding of procedural unconscionability where the rules affect only Plaintiff's procedural rights. ((2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1472.) Nor is the arbitration agreement substantively unconscionable. Plaintiff's argument as to lack of mutuality lacks merit because the agreement specifically limits its application to the employment relationship between Plaintiff and the signatory Defendant only, and the Confidentiality and Invention Assignment Agreement is inapplicable to employment related disputes. Further, the qualifying language in the attorneys' fees provision keeps it from running afoul of Armendariz's minimum requirement that "the arbitration agreement ... cannot generally require the employee to bear any type of expense that the employee would not be required to bear if he or she were free to bring the action in court." (Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83, 110-11.) Additionally, the mere possibility of the "repeat player effect"-without any evidence-is not enough to render an arbitration agreement unconscionable, and the agreement is not subject to unilateral modification or revocation. Finally, Plaintiff produces insufficient evidence to establish revocation of the original agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
